channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇪🇹 Ethiopian Election Expected to Hand Abiy Ahmed Landslide Win
item.description: Ethiopia holds parliamentary and regional elections with analysts predicting a landslide victory for PM Abiy Ahmed's party despite significant unrest across much of the country.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US Moves to Block Nvidia AI Chip Shipments to Chinese Firms Abroad
item.description: The Commerce Department issued new guidance to close a loophole allowing Chinese AI firms' overseas subsidiaries to access restricted US AI chips, tightening export controls.

item.title: 🇨🇴 Colombian Presidential Vote Heads to Runoff Between Leftist and Pro-Trump Candidate
item.description: Celebrity lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella, who admires Trump, will face leftist senator Iván Cepeda in a June 21 runoff after no candidate won outright in the first round.

item.title: 🇲🇲 Myanmar Village Explosion Kills at Least 55 People
item.description: A blast in Kaungtup village in northern Myanmar killed at least 55 and injured dozens more. A rebel army said it was caused by accidental explosion of mining materials.

item.title: 🇨🇿🇪🇺 Czech Republic Likely to Miss NATO Defence Spending Target
item.description: Prime Minister Andrej Babis told the Financial Times that the Czech Republic will probably fail to meet NATO's 2% GDP military spending target this year.

item.title: 🇨🇴 Pro-Trump Presidential Candidate Wins Spot in Colombian Runoff
item.description: Abelardo de la Espriella will face Iván Cepeda, who has sought to achieve "total peace" in the South American nation.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Tehran Reportedly Making Changes to Latest Draft of Mou After Trump Toughens US Stance
item.description: Iran is reportedly revising its latest draft MOU following Trump's tougher stance in ongoing negotiations.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan's Q1 Capex Stalls as Iran War Concerns Weigh on Business Confidence
item.description: Japanese business investment stalled in Q1 as concerns over the Iran conflict and global uncertainty weighed on corporate confidence.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Pushes Back Against Accusations of New Militarism
item.description: Japan is defending its defense policies amid regional tensions, pushing back against Chinese accusations of renewed militarism.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇵🇭 China Patrols Scarborough Shoal After Philippines Warns of Threat
item.description: China's military and coast guard carried out patrols near disputed waters in the South China Sea, a day after the Philippines warned of continued threat from Beijing.

item.title: 🇰🇷🇯🇵 South Korea and Japan Discuss Military Logistics Support Deal
item.description: Seoul confirmed discussions with Tokyo on a military logistics support agreement, deepening defense ties amid regional security concerns.

item.title: 🇸🇾 Syria's Sharaa Holds Phone Call With Trump, Syrian Presidency Says
item.description: Syria's president reportedly held a phone call with former US President Trump, according to the Syrian presidency.

item.title: 🇲🇩 Patriarch of One of Moldova's Two Competing Churches Steps Down
item.description: The patriarch of one of Moldova's two competing Orthodox churches has stepped down amid ongoing ecclesiastical tensions.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia-Appointed Official Says Child Killed, 11 Injured in Drone Attack
item.description: A Russian-appointed official in occupied Ukraine reported that a child was killed and 11 people injured in a drone attack.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Businesses Stay Downbeat About Economy, Surveys Show
item.description: British businesses remain pessimistic about the economic outlook as inflation and uncertainty continue to weigh on sentiment.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu Taps Shmuel Ben Ezra, Arrow-3 Innovator, as National Security Adviser
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu appointed Shmuel Ben Ezra, known for his work on the Arrow-3 missile defense system, as national security adviser.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Coast Guard Patrols Near Scarborough Shoal With Philippine Forces
item.description: US Coast Guard conducted patrols near Scarborough Shoal alongside Philippine forces amid concerns about potential Chinese build-up in the area.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Overseas Brokerage Investors Face Trading Crackdown
item.description: Chinese authorities are cracking down on offshore brokerage services used by mainland investors to trade overseas stocks.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel's Advance Into Southern Lebanon Cause for Serious Concern, German FM Says
item.description: Germany's foreign minister expressed serious concern about Israel's military advance into southern Lebanon amid ongoing regional tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Reopens Most Entrances to 18 Underground Missile Sites Struck in War
item.description: Iran has reopened most entrances to its underground missile facilities that were struck during the recent conflict, according to a report.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Castigates Europe Over Defence Spend, NATO Reassures Asia
item.description: The US criticized European NATO members over defense spending shortfalls as the alliance moved to reassure Asian partners about security commitments.

item.title: 🇧🇾 Belarus Leader Lukashenko Issues Warning Amid Russia-Ukraine Tensions
item.description: Belarusian President Lukashenko issued warnings about the ongoing conflict between Russia and Ukraine, with AP News analyzing the regional implications.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India's RBI Policy Decision in Focus as Iran War Impacts Rupee and Bonds
item.description: India's central bank monetary policy decision takes on heightened importance as the Iran war affects growth and inflation outlook through energy prices.

item.title: 💰 Dollar Steadies as Markets Await Signals on Iran War and Central Banks
item.description: The US dollar held steady after weekly losses as markets awaited Middle East peace talk results and signals on central bank rate decisions.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China's Crude Oil Imports Slump to Near 10-Year Low
item.description: China's seaborne crude imports fell to the lowest in almost 10 years as the Iran war disrupted operations in the world's biggest oil importer.
